# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cicd security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    parameters {
        string(name: 'ARTIFACT_URL', description: 'URL of the build artifact to download')
        string(name: 'PRODUCTION_SERVER', description: 'Production server to deploy the artifact')
    }

    stages {
        stage('Download Artifact') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def artifactUrl = params.ARTIFACT_URL
                    def artifactFileName = artifactUrl.tokenize('/').last()
                    
                    // Validate URL to prevent injection attacks
                    if (!artifactUrl.startsWith('http://') && !artifactUrl.startsWith('https://')) {
                        error('Invalid URL provided. Only HTTP/HTTPS URLs are allowed.')
                    }

                    // Download the artifact
                    sh "curl -O ${artifactUrl}"
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Deploy to Production') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def productionServer = params.PRODUCTION_SERVER
                    def artifactFileName = params.ARTIFACT_URL.tokenize('/').last()

                    // Validate server address
                    if (!productionServer.matches('^[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+$')) {
                        error('Invalid production server address.')
                    }

                    // Deploy the artifact
                    sh """
                        scp ${artifactFileName} user@${productionServer}:/path/to/deploy/
                        ssh user@${productionServer} 'deploy_script.sh /path/to/deploy/${artifactFileName}'
                    """
                }
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        always {
            cleanWs()
        }
    }
}
